name: write_file
description: Write content to a text file. This is the PRIMARY and RECOMMENDED method for creating or overwriting files - always use this instead of 'run_shell' with 'echo', 'tee', 'cat <<', or similar shell tricks when you need to write text to a file. This tool is purpose-built for safe, precise file writing without invoking a shell. Always prefer this tool over shell-based alternatives for creating configuration files, source code, logs, or any other text-based output.

path.description: The relative or absolute path where the file should be written. Any missing parent directories will typically be created automatically.

content.description: The complete text content to write into the file. Supports multi-line strings, code, JSON, markdown, or any text data.
